scenarios.description: children() returns empty Elements when no child nodes exist (childNodeSize==0)
scenarios.precondition: Element parent = new Element("div"); // no children appended

scenarios.description: children() returns one child and caches shadowChildrenRef on first call (childNodeSize==1, cache miss)
scenarios.precondition: Element parent = new Element("div"); parent.appendElement("span"); // one child element
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Elements list size is 1 and contains the appended <span> element

scenarios.description: children() returns multiple children and populates cache on first invocation (childNodeSize>1, cache miss, loop N)
scenarios.precondition: Element parent = new Element("ul"); parent.appendElement("li"); parent.appendElement("li"); // two child elements
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Elements list size is 2 containing both <li> children in order
pseudocode: assert result.size() == 2 && result.get(0) == c1 && result.get(1) == c2;

scenarios.description: children() returns cached children list on second call without mutation (cache hit, no rebuild)
scenarios.precondition: Element parent = new Element("div"); parent.appendElement("p"); // one child element
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Elements list from second call is same instance and still size 1

scenarios.description: children() rebuilds cache after mutation via appendChild (cache invalidation)
scenarios.precondition: Element parent = new Element("div"); parent.appendElement("em"); parent.children(); // build cache
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Elements list after append has size 2 and includes new <strong> child, different instance from first
pseudocode: assert second.size() == 2 && second.get(0) == c1 && second.get(1) == c2 && second != first;
